title: Luetkea caespitosa elatior
intro: Luetkea caespitosa elatior, commonly known as alpine lupine, is a perennial legume plant native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Luetkea caespitosa elatior include isoflavones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, such as lupinol and lupanine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly employed for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects.
taxonomy: Luetkea caespitosa elatior is classified as a subspecies of the genus Luetkea, which is a type of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This subspecies belongs to the species Luetkea caespitosa and is native to North America. The genus Luetkea is further classified under the family Rosaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the rose family. The classification of Luetkea caespitosa elatior can be expressed in a taxonomic hierarchy as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Class: Magnoliopsida, Order: Rosales, Family: Rosaceae, Genus: Luetkea, Species: Luetkea caespitosa, Subspecies: Luetkea caespitosa elatior. The classification of Luetkea caespitosa elatior is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics.
